terms.definition: Halting iterative training when a monitored validation metric stops improving for a configured number of passes. Canonical definition in this topic.
Tracking a validation metric (val/loss, val/f1, etc.) over epochs or rounds to detect when generalization stops improving while training metrics may still improve.
The count of consecutive evaluation passes without beating the best validation score before training stops — also called early_stopping_rounds in boosting libraries.
Reloading model parameters from the checkpoint saved at peak validation performance when stopping triggers — not from the final epoch.
In gradient boosting libraries, the round index with best validation performance — used for prediction instead of the full n_estimators count.
Minimum improvement required to reset the patience counter — filters insignificant noisy gains on validation metrics.
The validation scalar early stopping watches each pass — e.g. val/loss (minimize) or val/f1 (maximize) with mode matching the metric direction.
The pattern where training loss keeps improving while validation loss stalls or worsens — the primary signal that early stopping should trigger.
